Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Agricultural managers, farm operations staff, forestry supervisors, mining engineers, energy facility operators, GIS analysts, environmental compliance officers, data analysts in natural resources, operations managers in agriculture and energy sectors
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Environmental Compliance, Data Management, Agricultural Operations, Mining Operations, Energy Operations, GIS and Mapping, Risk Management
Description: Security and accuracy concerns in remote sensing and aerial monitoring.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how agricultural or resource industries operate, along with a general interest in data collection technologies such as drones or satellites, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key security and accuracy risks associated with satellite and drone data collection across agriculture, forestry, fishing, mining, and energy sectors. You will also be able to explain how these risks arise in remote sensing and aerial monitoring operations, and apply practical strategies to safeguard data integrity in your organisation.
